# EdgeBase Kotlin Multiplatform SDK

Use this file as a quick-reference contract for AI coding assistants working with the
public Kotlin Multiplatform SDK packages in this directory.

## Package Boundary

Use the Kotlin SDK only in trusted code.

The `:client` module is for app clients, the `:admin` module is for trusted JVM-side
service code, and the `:core` module provides the shared KMP runtime used by both.

## Source Of Truth

- Root README: https://github.com/edge-base/edgebase/blob/main/packages/sdk/kotlin/README.md
- Admin README: https://github.com/edge-base/edgebase/blob/main/packages/sdk/kotlin/admin/README.md
- SDK Overview: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks
- Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks/client-vs-server
- Admin Users: https://edgebase.fun/docs/authentication/admin-users
- Database Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/database/admin-sdk
- Push Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/push/admin-sdk
- Analytics Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/analytics/admin-sdk
- Admin SDK Reference: https://edgebase.fun/docs/admin-sdk/reference

## Public Artifacts

- JitPack group: `com.github.edge-base.edgebase`
- tags are versioned with the leading `v`, for example `v0.1.4`
- `com.github.edge-base.edgebase:edgebase-core:v0.1.4`
- `com.github.edge-base.edgebase:edgebase-client:v0.1.4`
- `com.github.edge-base.edgebase:edgebase-admin-kotlin:v0.1.4`
- the current public JitPack route validates the JVM publications of `:core` and `:client`
- do not recommend the root `edgebase-kotlin` artifact for public installs

## Canonical Examples

### Create a client

```kotlin
import dev.edgebase.sdk.client.ClientEdgeBase

val client = ClientEdgeBase("https://your-project.edgebase.fun")
```

### Query a table

```kotlin
val posts = client
    .db("shared")
    .table("posts")
    .where("status", "==", "published")
    .getList()
```

### Create an admin client

```kotlin
import dev.edgebase.sdk.admin.AdminEdgeBase

val admin = AdminEdgeBase(
    "https://your-project.edgebase.fun",
    serviceKey = System.getenv("EDGEBASE_SERVICE_KEY") ?: error("Missing EDGEBASE_SERVICE_KEY"),
)
```

### Manage users

```kotlin
val created = admin.adminAuth.createUser(
    email = "june@example.com",
    password = "pass1234",
    displayName = "June",
)
```

### Execute SQL

```kotlin
val rows = admin.sql(
    namespace = "workspace",
    instanceId = "ws-1",
    query = "SELECT * FROM documents WHERE status = ?",
    params = listOf("published"),
)
```

## Hard Rules

- keep Service Keys on trusted servers only
- `admin.adminAuth` is a property, not a method
- `db(namespace, instanceId = ...)` uses a named `instanceId` parameter in Kotlin
- `sql(namespace = ..., instanceId = ..., query = ..., params = ...)` is the canonical form
- `broadcast(channel, event, payload?)` is a server-side suspend function
- `edgebase-admin-kotlin` is JVM-only

## Common Mistakes

- do not use `edgebase-admin-kotlin` in Android UI code or browser bundles
- do not copy Java-style `adminAuth()` or JS-style `admin.auth`
- do not pass instance ids positionally when the code reads better with `instanceId = ...`
- do not call `sql("select 1")`; Kotlin expects the explicit admin entrypoint signature
- do not expose the Service Key through mobile or browser clients

## Quick Reference

```text
ClientEdgeBase(url)                                     -> ClientEdgeBase
AdminEdgeBase(url, serviceKey, projectId?)              -> AdminEdgeBase
client.auth                                             -> AuthClient
client.db(namespace, instanceId?)                       -> DbRef
client.analytics                                        -> AnalyticsClient
client.functions                                        -> FunctionsClient
client.storage                                          -> StorageClient
admin.adminAuth                                         -> AdminAuthClient
admin.db(namespace, instanceId?)                        -> DbRef
admin.sql(namespace, instanceId?, query, params)       -> suspend List<Map<String, Any?>>
admin.broadcast(channel, event, payload?)              -> suspend Unit
admin.destroy()                                         -> Unit
```
